questions.question: Define a sampling distribution and explain why it is a critical concept in statistical inference.
keyPoints: A sampling distribution is the probability distribution of a statistic (e.g., mean, proportion) obtained from a large number of samples drawn from a specific population.
It allows us to quantify the variability of the sample statistic and understand how it would behave if we repeated the sampling process many times.

It is the foundation for constructing confidence intervals and performing hypothesis tests, as it provides the distribution we use to calculate probabilities and make inferences about the population parameter.

questions.question: State the Central Limit Theorem (CLT) and describe its two most important implications for the sampling distribution of the mean.
keyPoints: The Central Limit Theorem states that for a sufficiently large sample size (n ≥ 30 is a common rule of thumb), the sampling distribution of the sample mean will be approximately normally distributed, regardless of the shape of the population distribution.
The mean of the sampling distribution (μ_x̄) will be equal to the population mean (μ).
The standard deviation of the sampling distribution, known as the standard error (σ_x̄), is equal to the population standard deviation (σ) divided by the square root of the sample size (σ_x̄ = σ/√n).

questions.question: A computer scientist is analyzing the average latency of a new network protocol. The population of latency times is heavily right-skewed with a mean of 150ms and a standard deviation of 45ms. If they collect a random sample of 50 measurements, describe the expected sampling distribution of the sample mean. Calculate the probability of observing a sample mean greater than 160ms.
keyPoints: The sampling distribution of the sample mean will be approximately normal (due to the CLT, as n = 50 ≥ 30) with a mean of 150ms and a standard error of 45/√50 ≈ 6.364ms.
To find the probability: Z = (160 - 150) / 6.364 ≈ 1.57. Using the standard normal distribution, P(Z > 1.57) ≈ 0.0582, or 5.82%.
